content.problemStatement:
Given a bubbleSort function that accepts an array as a parameter, sort the array using the **Bubble sort** algorithm.

**Note:** Do not write code to take input or print output, Only complete the implementation of the bubbleSort function.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the array sorted in non‑decreasing order on a single line. Numbers are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is sorted in ascending order using bubble sort.
The descending array becomes ascending after sorting.
A single‑element array is already sorted.

solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) – each test case performs at most N‑1 passes, each scanning up to N elements.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – sorting is done in‑place, only a few auxiliary variables are used.
